stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results (May 6, 2026): consolidated revenue Rs 8,860 cr (+26.9% YoY) — record high; PAT Rs 785.6 cr (+7% YoY). FY26 revenue Rs 28,884 cr (+29%); FY26 PAT Rs 2,708 cr (+32%). W&C segment +30% YoY, FMEG +47% YoY, international business +18%. Final dividend Rs 47/share (470%) recommended. Stock opened Rs 8,499, hit intraday high Rs 8,940 (+6%) on May 7 — 52-week high.
stocks.sector_context: Indian wires & cables sector remains in strong multi-year uptrend with ~13% revenue CAGR (FY19-FY24). Growth drivers: infrastructure capex, data centres, exports, solar/electrification. MOFSL projects 17% revenue CAGR and 16% EBIT CAGR FY26-FY28. Nifty Chemicals/industrials sentiment positive. Sector retains preferred-pick status despite UltraTech entry overhang.
stocks.peer_comparison: Polycab is sector leader (~26-27% organised W&C share) vs KEI ~9%, RR Kabel ~7%. Q4 result drove brokerage target raises: MOFSL & ICICI Securities up to Rs 9,800; JM Financial Rs 9,700; some targets to Rs 10,500. MOFSL maintains 'Buy' on Polycab while having downgraded KEI to 'Neutral' — Polycab is the sector preferred pick.
red_flags: Q4 PAT growth (+7%) materially trailed revenue (+26.9%) — margin compression visible
UltraTech Cement's W&C plant (Bharuch) commences Dec 2026 — competitive threat looming
Stock at fresh 52-week high — limited margin of safety on entry
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 47/share (record date pending AGM approval)
Multiple broker target upgrades up to Rs 9,800-10,500
FMEG +47% YoY growth indicates new revenue lever scaling
Multi-year tailwinds from solar, data centres, exports

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results (May 5, 2026): consolidated PAT Rs 4,668 cr (+42% YoY); revenue Rs 39,550 cr (+26%). FY26 consolidated PAT Rs 18,621 cr (+32%) on revenue Rs 1.98 trillion (+25%). Final dividend Rs 33/share (FV Rs 5) — up 30% YoY; record date July 3, 2026. April 2026 auto sales: total 94,627 units (+14% YoY); SUV 56,331 (+8%); tractors 46,404 (+20%); 3W +81%; CV 23,427. Stock around Rs 3,162 post-results.
stocks.sector_context: Indian auto sector strong in Q1 CY26: March SIAM data showed PV +16% YoY, 2W +19.3%, 3W +21.4%. April 2026 reinforced strength: Bajaj Auto +40%, M&M +14%. SUV mix and tractor cycle recovery driving sector. Featured in 'top stocks to buy' brokerage lists.
stocks.peer_comparison: M&M April total sales (+14%) trails Bajaj Auto (+40%) and is roughly in line with broader industry, but PAT growth (+42% consolidated) is best-in-class within the auto cohort. Tractor segment +20% indicates rural recovery; M&M dominates tractors. SUV +8% trails leader Bajaj's premium push but maintains share.
red_flags: April SUV growth (+8%) is moderating — base effect rising
Tractor cycle is mean-reverting; FY27 may see normalisation
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 33/share — record date July 3, 2026
Q4 PAT beat (+42%) — consensus upgrades likely
Tractor cycle still in upswing (+20% April)
SUV/EV pipeline including upcoming Born Electric launches

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results (May 6, 2026): standalone PAT Rs 2,746 cr (+34% YoY); revenue Rs 16,005 cr (+32%); EBITDA Rs 3,323 cr (+36%) — highest-ever quarterly profit. Board declared Rs 150/share dividend (1,500%) AND buyback up to Rs 5,633 cr at Rs 12,000/share (14.05% premium to May 6 close of Rs 10,314.60); 46.9 lakh shares = 1.68% of equity. April 2026 total sales 5,13,792 units (+40% YoY); total exports +83% to 2,65,582; 2W exports +78% to 2,29,890; domestic 2W +11%.
stocks.sector_context: Two-wheeler segment leading auto recovery with SIAM March data showing 2W +19.3% YoY. Export markets in strong recovery after multi-year drag — Bajaj 2W exports +78% in April. Premium 2W (KTM, Triumph), commercial 3W and EV (Chetak) supporting mix.
stocks.peer_comparison: Bajaj Auto best-in-class on April volume growth (+40% total) — well ahead of Hero MotoCorp, TVS Motor, Eicher. 2W export rebound (+78%) is unique in cohort. Combined dividend (Rs 150) + buyback at Rs 12,000 = strong cash return signal vs peers. However, Rs 150 dividend is lower than last year's Rs 210.
red_flags: Dividend Rs 150 lower YoY vs Rs 210 last year — partial offset by buyback
Buyback price Rs 12,000 may set a near-term ceiling for stock psychology
Domestic 2W growth (+11%) slower than export-led headline; sustainability tied to export demand
catalysts: Buyback up to Rs 5,633 cr at Rs 12,000/share (~17% premium to current Rs 10,224)
Dividend Rs 150/share
April 2026 sales +40% momentum carries into Q1 FY27
2W export ramp (+78%) reopening growth lever

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results (May 5-6, 2026): consolidated revenue Rs 4,615 cr (+7% YoY); EBITDA Rs 1,177 cr (+13.5%); PAT Rs 582 cr (+11%); EBITDA margin expanded to 25.5% (from 24.0%). FY26 PAT Rs 1,835 cr (+46.7%) on revenue Rs 15,786 cr (+7.4%). Specialty Chemicals division +23% growth. Board approved Rs 2,300 cr investment in new Odisha plant (HFOs and VHF for next-gen refrigerants) and Rs 88 cr Dahej HFC capacity expansion to 65,000 TPA. Indore project deferred. Stock surged ~9% on May 6 to intraday high Rs 2,762; trading ~Rs 2,737 on May 7.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty Chemicals index rose nearly 3% on May 6, 2026 driven by SRF, Pidilite, HSCL — sector sentiment improving from earlier H1 FY26 muted outlook. Long-term India chemicals production forecast remains +10.9%. Refrigerant gas pricing recovery and next-gen HFO/VHF demand supporting outlook.
stocks.peer_comparison: SRF specialty chemicals +23% growth and FY26 PAT +46.7% well ahead of most peers (Aarti Industries, Navin Fluorine, PI Industries). Rs 2,300 cr Odisha capex is one of the largest in the cohort. Featured in MOFSL May 7, 2026 target list alongside Polycab, Tata Steel, PB Fintech, Hero Moto. Stock now ~17% above 52-week low, valuation re-rating on capex visibility.
red_flags: Indore project deferred — flags capex prioritisation/return concerns
Topline growth modest (+7% YoY) despite strong margin expansion
Citi historically maintained 'Sell' — bearish broker stance lingers despite results
catalysts: Rs 2,300 cr Odisha plant for HFOs/VHF — multi-year visibility into next-gen refrigerants
Dahej HFC expansion to 65,000 TPA — commissioning within months
Specialty chemicals growth +23% — best-in-class in cohort
MOFSL target list inclusion (May 7, 2026)
FY26 PAT +46.7% — sharp earnings momentum

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results: standalone PAT Rs 19,221 cr (+9.1% YoY); FY26 credit growth 12%, deposit growth 14.4% to Rs 31.05 lakh cr (deposits outpacing loans). Gross advances Rs 29.6 lakh cr (+12%) — retail +6.5%, SME +17.2%, corporate/wholesale +13%. Asset quality improved: GNPA 1.15% (from 1.33%); NNPA 0.42%; ex-agri GNPA 0.91%. CAR 19.71%, CET-1 17.3%. Final dividend Rs 13/share + Rs 2.50 special interim (Aug 2025) = Rs 15.50 total FY26. Stock Rs 797.6 on May 6 — 21.84% below 52-week high, 9.76% above 52-week low.
stocks.sector_context: Banking sector asset quality at multi-decade best with system NPAs near 20-year lows. Bank Nifty index volatile but constructive; HDFC Bank is largest constituent at 25.83%. Sector beneficiary of credit growth (+16% system) and deposit normalization, though private banks face margin pressure.
stocks.peer_comparison: ICICI Bank Q1 FY26 PAT +15.5% YoY to Rs 12,760 cr — bottom-line growth materially ahead of HDFC Bank's +9.1%. ICICI NNPA 0.37% vs HDFC's 0.42% — ICICI marginally better asset quality. MOFSL/Equentis comparisons (Mar 2026) flag ICICI as preferred pick on growth, efficiency, and digital. HDFC Bank offers higher dividend yield (~1.1-1.2%). Stock has lagged peers; ICICI delivered 14% CAGR while HDFC's 1Y return is negative.
red_flags: Stock down ~22% from 52-week high — material underperformance vs sector
Q4 PAT growth +9.1% trails ICICI Bank (+15.5%) and system credit growth (+16%)
Deposit growth (+14.4%) outpacing loans (+12%) — drag on NIM in near term
Brokerage consensus prefers ICICI Bank over HDFC Bank
catalysts: Dividend Rs 13 final + Rs 2.50 special = Rs 15.50/share FY26
Asset quality improvement (GNPA 1.15%, ex-agri 0.91%)
Strong CAR 19.71% supports growth/payout flexibility
Stock near 52-week low base — valuation cushion
HDFC Ltd merger integration nearing completion — re-rating optionality
